method: Independent walkthrough of the audit report against evidence the orchestrator gathered separately: state.json empty rosters (read directly before the audit ran), 10 untracked files (git status), BOM fix (authored and committed in 8ba7278), remote head verified via gh repo view.

scenario_results.note: Every ledger claim traced to git/file evidence incl. live remote check via git ls-remote
Under-gated acceptance (D1) and dead state fields (D2) flagged as unsupported/misleading progress
Eight discrepancies listed with exact paths and code line references

orchestrator_dispositions.D1: Decision recorded: task packets may narrow config-default gates when a gate family is not applicable (G1 covers external official sources; M0-T000/T001/T003 have none). Future packets must state the narrowing rationale in the G0 readiness record.
orchestrator_dispositions.D2: Fixed: sync_state() added to project_control.py and called from claim/progress/submit/gate/accept.
orchestrator_dispositions.D3: Fixed: full ledger committed and pushed in the bootstrap follow-up commit; checkpoint CP-0003 records it.
orchestrator_dispositions.D4: Acknowledged: enrich_bootstrap_tasks.py was an orchestrator action (authorized to edit task packets); script retained in tools/ and committed for the evidence trail. forbidden_paths bind producers, not the orchestrator.
orchestrator_dispositions.D5: Fixed: gate() now preserves prior gate results in a history array instead of overwriting.
orchestrator_dispositions.D6: Adopted: future producer reports must embed actual command outputs.
orchestrator_dispositions.D7: Accepted as code-consistent behavior (progress log records only explicit progress calls).
orchestrator_dispositions.D8: Accepted as minor; checkpoint commit field remains free text for pre-git edge cases.
